hero.heading: Tackle nature's toughest monitoring challenges
hero.description: Global Nature Watch is an open, AI-powered system that transforms groundbreaking land monitoring data into intelligence to understand Earth's landscapes. Test the preview and help shape the future of land monitoring.
hero.previewTooltip: This is an early version of Global Nature Watch. Our agent may make mistakes and some features are still in development. Your usage and feedback will help us enhance it!

features.heading: Get answers to your toughest questions about landscapes, backed by data
features.description: Global Nature Watch is testing new ways to make geospatial information easier to use. Try asking in plain language and explore the insights it can provide.

tabs.label: Explore trusted data with an AI assistant
tabs.description: Ask a question in plain language and our assistant will suggest the most useful available datasets and analyses for your work.
tabs.caption: Quickly find the most relevant data for your work.

tabs.label: Tailored answers to your context
tabs.description: Explore how Global Nature Watch's assistant can shape responses to your needs, from comparing regions to highlighting local patterns that may be most relevant to your work.
tabs.caption: Shape responses to your needs.

tabs.label: Insights you can act on
tabs.description: Global Nature Watch's assistant helps translate analyses into clear takeaways. It offers a starting point for reports, policies or field decisions while opening the door to dive deeper.
tabs.caption: Generate clear takeaways from complex data.

supportWork.heading: Built to support how you work
supportWork.description: Whether you're running field programs, advising governments, leading research, or driving business sustainability, Global Nature Watch can help you start from data, not from scratch.

howItWorks.heading: How it works
howItWorks.userQuestion: Where are the most disturbances to nature happening now?

steps.title: Processing your intent
steps.description: When you ask Global Nature Watch a question, our system of agents work together to understand your request and deliver the most accurate, relevant answers.

steps.title: Retrieving quality data
steps.description: Our data comes via APIs from Global Forest Watch and Land & Carbon Lab, platforms powered by contributions from researchers and partners around the globe. This means verifiable data from authoritative sources.

steps.title: Tuning the AI model's response
steps.description: We use Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to link data retrieved via our trusted APIs with real documentation, methods papers and metadata from our research.

steps.title: Returning a response
steps.description: Our agents create summaries of spatial data, search through datasets, and explain insights clearly in over 170 languages.

futureMonitoring.heading: The future of ecosystem monitoring
futureMonitoring.description: Global Nature Watch has been in development since 2024 and is now being shared as a preview. We'll keep adding data and features, but we'd love for you to be part of what comes next.

newEraQuote.quote: It's a pivotal moment for the monitoring of our natural world.
newEraQuote.attribution: A new era of ecosystem intelligence

cta.heading: How will you use monitoring intelligence?
cta.description: Join the future of ecosystem monitoring and help us shape what comes next.

trustedPlatforms.heading: Built on trusted data from leading platforms
